purpose: Document why RoboChallenge pi and ReKep cannot be fairly scored on RoboLab-120 until adapters exist.
comparison_rule: Only policies that emit RoboLab-compatible Franka/Robotiq actions from RoboLab observations can enter success-rate tables as real results. Others are marked adapter_required.

main_mismatch: RoboChallenge baseline is built around its own task/data interface.
Existing checkpoint path is a Table30v2/ALOHA style candidate, not a RoboLab Pi0-family policy arg.
RoboLab expects per-step observations with over-shoulder camera, wrist camera, proprio, prompt, and Franka/Robotiq action chunks.

input: robot joint positions and gripper state
output: RoboLab action vector or action chunk compatible with policies/pi0_family/run.py control loop
adapter_contract.promotion_gate: Only after one full episode writes video, HDF5, event log, and episode_results.jsonl.

main_mismatch: ReKep is a perception/planning/control route, not a direct learned VLA action policy.
It needs keypoint extraction, constraint generation, subgoal optimization, and a RoboLab low-level controller.
Its result should be reported as planner baseline, not mixed silently with Pi05/PaliGemma policy baselines.

output: same episode_results.jsonl/HDF5/video evidence contract as VLA policies
adapter_contract.first_smoke: A single pick-place task with visible target object and container.
adapter_contract.promotion_gate: Only after planner actions are executed in Isaac, not merely generated offline.
